div.p: sha256_compress(H, block[0]) ① sha256_message_schedule → W[0..63] ② a..h ← H[0..7] ③ 64 rounds: T1, T2, rotate ④ H[i] += working_var[i] ← feed-forward H modified in place
H[0..7] (updated) H is the same array — modified in place by += Carries entropy of all 56 message bytes
sha256_compress(H, block[1]) ① sha256_message_schedule → W[0..63] ② a..h ← H[0..7] ← from block[0] output ③ 64 rounds: T1, T2, rotate ④ H[i] += working_var[i] H modified in place again
Big-endian byte extraction digest[i×4+0] = H[i] >> 24 digest[i×4+1] = H[i] >> 16 digest[i×4+2] = H[i] >> 8 digest[i×4+3] = H[i] (NOT memcpy — endianness-safe)

Merkle-Damgård Chain H is the same uint32_t[8] array throughout both calls. sha256_compress() modifies H in place via H[i] += working_var[i]. Block 1 starts from Block 0's output state — not from SHA256_INITIAL_HASH. This cumulative chaining is what makes the final digest depend on every input bit.
